PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-20-2021 Continued To Nov-08-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Scott Fraser appeared by zoom for Marina Donskoy.
Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for and with Leo Bautista.
John Saadeh and Nathan Pastor appeared by zoom for Tatiana Donskoy.

The court questioned whether this is an ancillary probate, or a main probate and noted that the petition for probate of will filed by Tatiana has no original will, nor a certified copy of a will, and that all of the documents for Mr. Fraser's petition need to have certified translations. Mr. Saadeh stated that this is a main probate and then stated that he was not able to get the original will because the estate is being managed in Russia. Mr. Saadeh also stated that he got a certified copy of the will and submitted it, that he republished with the original name and the aka, and that he took care of everything that was in the Probate Examiner's notes. Mr. Pastor stated that in Russia the notary who notarizes the will acts as a government official who manages the assets, that there is no real property in San Francisco, that there are two museums and that they are managed as corporations. Mr. Fraser stated that his filing is a competing petition, that his documents have been translated, and that at the bottom of each page it indicates who it was translated by and has the contact information. Mr. Fraser also stated that the issue of there being no name in the caption was a formatting issue, that the publication and the notice contained all of the information, and that it seems to that all of the documents have English information on them and notations on the bottom. In addition, Mr. Fraser stated that there is one will, that it is the same will, that there are two different translations, that he does not believe there is a dispute over the document, and that the dispute is over who will be the administrator. Mr. Pastor stated that they are proposing the nominee of the surviving spouse who is a close friend and the CPA, and that there is an issue of priority to nominate. The Petitions for Probate and for Letters Testamentary are continued to November 8, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court suggested that Fraser address the formatting issue.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
